## Development arc (curated)

B2 carries Proposition A — the layer-margin inequalities that the whole
Sector-B positivity chain rests on — as a conditional theorem at T6 on the
named hypothesis set {H-LAYER, H-A0}. The migration chain revalidated the
Proposition-A evidence; the note set demonstrates the versioned-reissue and
standard-form disciplines.

The 2026-06-06 session opened the H-A0 removal pathway (ha0-removal): H-A0's
uniqueness + zero-at-gap content reduces to a sign-decomposition theorem
skeleton plus one anchor-window inequality (m*/m_w ≈ ×7.8), with the proposed
replacement H-A0 → H-ANCHOR and named gaps G-A0-DUI, G-A0-VER. This is a T3
proof sketch pending the registered follow-up script; no tier change yet.

The H-A0 removal pathway was then EXECUTED (2026-06-06, operator-authorized): the A=0 uniqueness and zero-at-gap content of H-A0 is now a sign-decomposition theorem — F_0'(m) = (1/2)M'(m)g(m) with M'<0 (L1), g strictly decreasing on the window (L2), g<0 beyond it (L3), and a unique zero g(m*)=0 — conditional only on the single closed-form anchor inequality m* > m_w (H-ANCHOR, x7.76 margin) and the textbook DUI regularity (G-A0-DUI). The sign decomposition is machine-verified (ha0_sign_decomposition.py 14/14; G-A0-VER closed). H-A0 was REPLACED by H-ANCHOR + G-A0-DUI in both B2 and B1: a strictly weaker, explicit hypothesis from which the quadrature-scheme curve-shape certification and the 5.5e-3 scheme systematic exit the load-bearing chain. Both T6 theorems strengthen; no tier number changes.
